home.hero_subtitle: Visualize the subtle links connecting theorems, concepts, and proofs. Deepen your knowledge and participate in this collaborative mapping.

concepts.title: Mathematical Concepts
concepts.desc: Explore the complete list of available mathematical concepts, theorems, and definitions.

categories.title: Categories
categories.desc: Discover the structural organization of the different fields and branches of mathematics.

mathematicians.title: Mathematicians
mathematicians.desc: Browse the biographies and timelines of the illustrious minds who forged the field.

types.title: Mathematical Types
types.desc: Consult classifications according to the nature of propositions (theorem, lemma, axiom...).

contribution.title: A passion for mathematics?
contribution.desc: Contribute by correcting a typo, enriching a statement, or adding new concepts. Every contribution makes the project grow!

footer.description: An open-source platform to explore and understand mathematics through their connections.

concept.verified: Concept verified and approved by moderation

auth.login_subtitle: Enter your credentials to access your account.
auth.login_desc: Log in to explore interactive proofs, add favorites, and contribute to building the collaborative mapping of mathematical knowledge.
auth.forgot_password_desc: Enter your email address to receive reset instructions.
auth.forgot_password_success: If an account exists with this email address, you will soon receive an email with instructions to reset your password.
auth.new_password_success: Your password has been successfully changed. You will be redirected to the login page.
auth.error_default: An error occurred. Please try again.
auth.username_min_length: Username must contain at least 3 characters
auth.username_regex: Only letters, numbers, and underscores are allowed
auth.password_min_length: Password must contain at least 8 characters
auth.password_uppercase: Password must contain at least one uppercase letter
auth.password_number: Password must contain at least one number
auth.register_desc: Create an account to start submitting corrections, proposing proofs, and actively participating in the community.
auth.register_subtitle: Create your credentials to join the project.

entities.type_desc_1: This type regroups all mathematical concepts classified as

about.title: About MathGraph
about.description: This project was created to provide a clear and intuitive visualization of the mathematical universe. Each node represents a fundamental theorem or concept, making it easy to explore and understand the close connections linking different branches of mathematics.
about.value1_desc: Visualize theorems and concepts not in isolation, but as an interconnected network of mathematical knowledge.
about.value2_desc: Each sheet provides precise LaTeX statements and detailed proofs to combine scientific rigor with visual accessibility.
about.value3_desc: Knowledge is built together. Fix a typo, propose an improvement, or add new sheets to grow the map.
about.call_to_action: Developed with passion, this project is constantly evolving. Your feedback and contributions are welcome!

lost.description: Sorry, the page you are looking for does not exist or has been moved.

support.title: Support MathGraph
support.description: MathGraph is a completely free and open-source project. Your support helps us keep the servers online, continuously improve the interface, and expand the universe of interactive demonstrations.
support.tier1_desc: Contribute to hosting costs, computation servers for the interactive graph, and domain name.
support.tier2_desc: Link your developer profile to the project repository and gain direct visibility in the README file.
support.tier3_desc: No financial participation required! Help develop the code or write mathematical sheets.
support.thanks: Every gesture, whether it's a financial donation, development time, or sharing the site, touches our hearts. Thank you!

contribution.title: Contribution Guide
contribution.description: Thank you for wanting to enrich MathGraph! To ensure the map of mathematical knowledge remains reliable and pleasant to consult, here are a few simple rules.
contribution.footer: 🚀 Every correction or addition, however small, helps the entire mathematical community!
contribution.do_1: Fix an error or typo (spelling, explanations...)
contribution.do_2: Add examples, clarifications, or reliable references
contribution.do_3: Improve text clarity without making it too complex
contribution.do_4: Create a new sheet on a relevant topic
contribution.dont_2: Delete someone else's work without reason
contribution.dont_3: Add jokes, opinions, or off-topic content
contribution.dont_4: Promote a site, product, or service
contribution.tip_2: If you're unsure, propose it: the moderation team will help you.
contribution.tip_3: Be kind and respectful to other contributors.

recent_changes.title: Modification History
recent_changes.description: Discover the evolution of the mathematical knowledge base, in full transparency.

settings.title: Site Settings
settings.maintenance_desc: Enabling maintenance mode temporarily redirects all non-administrative visitors to an offline information page.
settings.recalculate_desc: Forces the backend physics engine (ForceAtlas2) to recalculate the positions of all nodes in the graph. This operation may take a few seconds.
settings.recalculate_success: The graph has been successfully recalculated!
